_comment: DO NOT EDIT: FILE GENERATED AUTOMATICALLY BY PTERODACTYL PANEL - PTERODACTYL.IO
name: NetworkScan
author: loki@crazycoder.dev
description: Given a set of subnets, tests the available attack surface your Pterodactyl Panel containers can see. Useful for testing firewall isolation rules.

installation.script:
#!/bin/sh
set -eu
DEST="${1:-/mnt/server}"
apk add --no-cache nmap
mkdir -p "$DEST/lib" "$DEST/results"
cp /usr/bin/nmap "$DEST/"
cp -r /usr/share/nmap "$DEST/share"
ldd /usr/bin/nmap | awk '/=>/ {print $3}' | xargs cp -t "$DEST/lib/"
cat > "$DEST/scan.sh" <<'EOF'
#!/bin/ash
export LD_LIBRARY_PATH=lib
export NMAPDIR=share

./nmap -v --stats-every 15s -sT -Pn -n -p- -T4 --min-parallelism 1000 --max-parallelism 10000 --open -oN results/scan_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).txt ${SCAN_TARGETS}

while IFS= read -r line; do
    case "$line" in
        "STOP") exit 0 ;;
    esac
done
EOF
chmod +x "$DEST/scan.sh"

variables.name: SCAN_TARGETS
variables.description: Space-separated list of IPs, hostnames, CIDR ranges, or octet ranges
